g.title: Concentrated mass added to a rotor node. Has no stiffness or damping of its own. Useful for modeling extra masses such as couplings, impellers, bolts, etc.
Application examples: - Coupling mass at an end node - Impeller mass not modeled as DiskElement - Bolt, key, balance weight mass - Mass correction at a specific node
Node number where the mass is added. Must correspond to an existing rotor node.
m: concentrated mass [kg] Applies the same mass in x and y directions. Simplest form of usage.
mx: mass in x direction [kg] my: mass in y direction [kg] mz: mass in z direction [kg] (axial) Allows different masses per direction. Used when m is not specified.
Mass matrix [4×4] Diagonal: [mx, my, 0, 0] Translation only, no rotary inertia
Gyroscopic matrix [4×4] Null matrix (no gyroscopic effect)

mx: mass in x direction [kg] my: mass in y direction [kg] mz: mass in z direction [kg] (axial) Allows different masses per direction. Used when m is not specified.
Mass matrix [4×4] Diagonal: [mx, my, 0, 0] Translation only, no rotary inertia
Gyroscopic matrix [4×4] Null matrix (no gyroscopic effect)
